name: Cairo Governorate (Egypt)

description: A light blue field bearing a central white disc edged dark blue, within which a dark blue silhouette of the Cairo Citadel and minarets stands before a stylised golden sun.
history: The flag of the Cairo Governorate, which is coterminous with the city of Cairo. The governorate emblem — a silhouette of the Citadel of Saladin and the mosques of Islamic Cairo — was redesigned in 2010, placing the landmarks before a stylised golden sun on a white disc set on a light blue field. It replaced the earlier plain citadel-on-light-blue design.
